title: Cirsium × tataricum
intro: Cirsium × tataricum, also known as Tatar flower thistle or Tatar weed, is a hybrid plant species within the thistle family (Asteraceae) that is native to Eurasia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which can aid in alleviating symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cirsium × tataricum include flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in tinctures and salves, to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Cirsium × tataricum is a hybrid species of thistle, classified as a cross between the Russian thistle (Silybum marianum) and the field thistle (Cirsium vulgare). This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. The '×' symbol in its name indicates that it is a hybrid species. Cirsium × tataricum is a perennial plant native to Eurasia, and its classification helps distinguish it from other thistle species. Its unique genetic makeup makes it a distinct species within the Cirsium genus.
uses: Cirsium × tataricum, commonly known as the Tatar thistle or Nodding thistle, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues. Cirsium × tataricum is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and gout. The plant's roots and flowers are used in herbal remedies, often in combination with other plants to enhance their medicinal effects. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cirsium × tataricum for medicinal purposes.

properties: Cirsium × tataricum has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various therapeutic properties. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and protect the skin from infections and irritation. It is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion, due to its ability to regulate bowel movements and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. constituents: Cirsium × tataricum contains a variety of active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have been identified to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The sesquiterpene lactones, specifically cnicin and tatarinic acid, have been found to have potential anticancer and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids may contribute to its antioxidant and protective effects on the cardiovascular system. Further research is needed to fully understand the pharmacological effects of these active constituents.

preparations: Cirsium × tataricum, also known as Tatarian thistle or subalpine thistle, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its purported health benefits. Herbal preparations made from its flowers and leaves have been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. side_effects: Cirsium × tataricum, also known as the Tatar thistle, can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Handling the plant's thistle-like leaves can lead to skin inflammation, redness, and itching. Ingesting any part of the plant may cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea. The sap of the Tatar thistle has been reported to cause skin and mucous membrane irritation. Overall, caution is advised when handling Cirsium × tataricum to avoid potential side effects.
